definition: Zero of eight historical commodity deficit forecasts of comparable magnitude have materialized, establishing a base rate that demands rigorous downward calibration of any headline supply deficit projection. This is a base rate, not a prediction — it does not mean the current deficit will not materialize.
evidence: 0/8 historical materialization rate confirmed as 'the single most empirically grounded finding in the entire knowledge base'
Applied to copper: headline 5-8Mt forecasts calibrated to 2-4Mt; base rate suggests further revision warranted
open_questions: Is the current copper deficit structurally different from prior deficit forecasts due to energy transition demand?
At what point does the base rate itself become misleading if structural conditions have genuinely changed?
